title: METHODS AND COMPOSITIONS FOR ON-DEMAND RELEASE OF ClO2 GAS FROM UV-ACTIVATED CHLORITE ION
abstract: Compositions and methods for generating ClO 2 gas are disclosed. A composition that includes a chlorite salt is activated by exposure to ultraviolet light. After an optional storage period, the composition is then exposed to moisture, resulting in the generation of ClO 2 gas. Exemplary compositions include polymers in which the chlorite salt is dispersed. The polymers may be used to form films that can be used to package, e.g., food products, pharmaceutical products, medical devices, and/or laboratory devices. Upon exposure to ultraviolet light and moisture, the packaging releases controlled quantities of ClO 2 gas, which may disinfect and/or deodorize the packaged device or product.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  WE CLAIM: 1. A method for generating chlorine dioxide from a composition comprising a polymer and a plurality of chlorite ions disposed in the polymer, the method comprising: exposing the composition to ultraviolet (UV) light; and exposing the composition to moisture. 2. A method according to claim 1, wherein exposing the composition to ultraviolet light comprises exposing the composition to UV light having a wavelength in the range of about 240 nm to 280 nm. 3. A method according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the composition is substantially free of an energy-activated catalyst and is substantially free of an acid-releasing compound. 4. A method according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the composition comprises less than 10% by weight of an energy-activated catalyst and comprises 2% or less by weight of an acid-releasing agent. 5. A method according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the composition comprises less than 5% by weight of an energy-activated catalyst and comprises 1% or less by weight of an acid-releasing agent. 6. A method according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the composition comprises less than 2% by weight of an energy-activated catalyst and comprises 1% or less by weight of an acid-releasing agent. 7. A method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the plurality of chlorite ions are present in the composition in the form of a chlorite salt. 8. A method according to claim 7, wherein the chlorite salt is sodium chlorite or potassium chlorite. 9. A method according to claim 7 or claim 8, wherein the composition comprises a weight ratio of an energy-activated catalyst to chlorite salt of 1 :5 or less and comprises a weight ratio of an acid-releasing compound to chlorite salt of 1 : 10 or less. A method according to claim 7 or claim 8, wherein the composition comprises a weight ratio of an energy-activated catalyst to chlorite salt of 1 : 10 or less and comprises a weight ratio of an acid-releasing compound to chlorite salt of 1 :20 or less. 11. A method according to any of one of claims 1-10, wherein exposing the composition to moisture comprises exposing the composition to humidified gas having a relative humidity within the range of about 20% to 100%. A method according to any one of claims 1-11, wherein the step of exposing the composition to UV light and the step of exposing the composition to moisture are performed simultaneously. A method according to any one of claims 1-11, wherein the step of exposing the composition to UV light is performed prior to the step of exposing the composition to moisture. A method according to claim 13, wherein the step of exposing the composition to UV light and the step of exposing the composition to moisture are separated by an intervening period of time, wherein the intervening period of time is at least one minute. A method according to claim 14, wherein the intervening period of time is at least hour. 16. A method according to claim 14, wherein the intervening period of time is within the range of about one hour to about 24 hours. 17. A method according to any one of claims 1-16, wherein the step of exposing the composition to ultraviolet light, the step of exposing the composition to moisture, or both, are repeated one or more times. 18. A method according to any one claims 1-17, further comprising drying the composition so that the composition has the moisture content of less than 500 ppm 19. A method according to claim 18, wherein the drying occurs prior to the step of exposing the composition to UV light. 20. A method according to any one of claims 1-17, wherein the composition has a moisture content of less than 500 ppm prior to the step of exposing the composition to moisture. 21. A method according to any one of claims 1-20, wherein a packaging film comprises the composition. 22. A method according to claim 21, wherein the packaging film is a multilayer packaging film and the composition forms a layer of the multilayer packaging film. 23. A method according to claim 21 or claim 22, wherein a package comprises the packaging film. 24. A method according to claim 23, wherein an article is disposed in the package. 25. A method according to claim 24, wherein the article is a laboratory device or a medical device. 26. A method according to any one of claims 1-20, wherein an article comprises the composition. 27. A method according to claim 26, wherein the article is a laboratory device, a medical device, or a food service device. 28. A method according to claim 26, wherein the article is a glove, a catheter or a cutting board. 29. A composition comprising: a polymer; and a plurality of chlorite ions (CIO 2 " ) dispersed in the polymer, wherein the composition is capable of generating C10 2 gas after being exposed to a two- stage gas generation protocol comprising a first stage comprising exposure of the chlorite ions to ultraviolet light and a second stage comprising exposure of the chlorite ions to moisture, and wherein the composition comprises less than 10% by weight of an energy-activated catalyst and comprises 2% or less by weight of an acid-releasing agent. 30. A composition according to claim 29, wherein the composition comprises less than 5% by weight of an energy-activated catalyst and comprises 1% or less by weight of an acid- releasing agent. 31. A composition according to claim 29, wherein the composition comprises less than 2% by weight of an energy-activated catalyst and comprises 1% or less by weight of an acid- releasing agent. 32. A composition comprising: a polymer; and a plurality of chlorite ions dispersed in the polymer, wherein the plurality of chlorite ions are in the form of a chlorite salt, 

 wherein the composition is capable of generating C10 2 gas after being exposed to a two- stage gas generation protocol comprising a first stage comprising exposure of the chlorite ions to ultraviolet light and a second stage comprising exposure of the chlorite ions to moisture, and wherein the composition comprises a weight ratio of an energy-activated catalyst to chlorite salt of 1 :5 or less and comprises a weight ratio of an acid-releasing compound to chlorite salt of 1 : 10 or less. 33. A composition according to claim 35, wherein the weight of the dispersed chlorite salt is at least 0.1% of the total weight of the composition. 37. A composition according to any one of claims 29-37, wherein the polymer is selected from the group consisting of a melt processable polymer, an extrudable polymer, a polyethylene, a polypropylene, a polyolefin, an ethylene vinyl acetate copolymer, a polyvinyl chloride, an ionomer, a polyamide, a polyester, a polyvinyl alcohol, a polylactic acid, a polyvinylidene chloride, and copolymers and mixtures thereof. 39. A laboratory or medical device comprising the composition of any one of claims 29-38. 40. A laboratory or medical device according to claim 39, wherein the device is a catheter or a glove. 41. A film comprising a composition according to any one of claims 29-38. 42. A packaging material comprising a film according to claim 41. 43. A packaged article comprising a packaging material according to claim 42 and an article disposed in the packaging material. 44. A composition according to any one of claims 29-38, wherein the composition is a solid. 45. A composition according to any one of claims 29-38, wherein the composition is a liquid. 46. A packaging film according to claim 64, wherein the film is a multilayer film. 66. A packaging film according to claim 65, further comprising a UV reflective layer. 67.
